Title
Entity Component System Prompt

Purpose
Introduce a lightweight ECS-inspired structure for Shadow Shift entities so player, enemies, pickups, hazards, projectiles, and effects can scale without turning into hardcoded one-off objects.

Why This Matters for V2
As Shadow Shift adds more enemy types, interactables, room objects, and combat rules, direct bespoke object logic will become expensive to maintain. A lightweight ECS-style layer improves extensibility without forcing an engine rewrite.

Detailed Codex Instructions
Context:
Shadow Shift is a vanilla JavaScript HTML5 canvas game. It already has working gameplay systems and should remain playable during the upgrade.

Goal:
Design and implement a minimal ECS-style entity architecture that coexists with the existing game structure and can gradually absorb current object types.

Implementation Steps:
1. Introduce an entity registry that stores entities by id and type.
2. Define a lightweight component model using plain objects. Components should be simple data containers such as:
   - transform
   - physics
   - collider
   - renderable
   - health
   - hurtbox
   - hitbox
   - AI state
   - elemental state
   - shadow state
3. Add systems that iterate over relevant entities each frame, such as movement, collision, combat, and rendering preparation.
4. Migrate one or two representative categories first, such as pickups/effects and one enemy family, before touching everything.
5. Keep the player and bosses on compatibility wrappers if a full migration would be risky.
6. Provide helper factories to build entities cleanly.
7. Document how old runtime objects map to ECS components.

Constraints:
- No full rewrite
- Use plain JavaScript objects and arrays/maps only
- Avoid class-heavy abstractions
- Keep the current gameplay running
- Maintain performance and readability

Implementation Notes
- This should be a pragmatic ECS, not a textbook engine framework.
- Favor incremental adoption.
- Systems should remain deterministic and easy to debug.
- Avoid deep inheritance; composition should do most of the work.

Validation Checklist
- Entities can be created, updated, and removed safely
- At least one current gameplay category uses the new ECS-style path
- Rendering and collisions still behave correctly
- The migration path for more entity types is obvious
- The new structure reduces special-case logic rather than increasing it
